title: Retry transient failures with capped backoff and jitter
description: Retry only retryable errors a bounded number of times with exponential backoff and randomized jitter to avoid synchronized storms.
body: Use when a call can fail transiently (timeouts, 503, connection reset). Do not retry immediately in a tight loop or retry non-retryable errors (4xx like 400/401/404), and do not let many clients retry in lockstep. Actions: classify errors as retryable or not, cap the attempt count and total time, use exponential backoff with full jitter, and respect Retry-After when present. Validation signals include a test showing bounded attempts, growing randomized delays, and no retry on a 400. Risk boundary: unbounded or un-jittered retries can amplify an outage into a thundering herd; combine with a circuit breaker and escalate if a dependency is failing widely.
